comment_text.sn2.30:1.3: Most of these individuals are unknown elsewhere. | For detailed presentations of the sectarian teachings that follow, see DN 2.
comment_text.sn2.30:2.6: This is regarded as a pernicious wrong view, which results in rebirth in hell. This shows the complexity of kamma, as Asama must have done some other good kamma to be reborn as a godling.
comment_text.sn2.30:3.1: A group of gods of this name (meaning “with plough”?) appeared alongside Vishnu at DN 20:14.1.
comment_text.sn2.30:8.1: Pakudhaka Kātiyāna is a variant spelling of Pakudha Kaccāyana.
comment_text.sn2.30:15.1: The celebration of Namuci’s defeat by Māṇavagāmiya is a striking verbal allusion to Rig Veda 5.30.7c, where the defeat of Namuci opened the way for “the progress of Manu” (manave gātum, Rig Veda 5.30.7c), i.e. the spread of the Aryan peoples who were Manu’s children. Reading Pali gāmiya as a secondary derivation from gama, “going”, yields the sense, “The Progress of Manu’s Children”. Here the term can have the Buddhist sense of the path of the Aryans.

root_text.sn2.30:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn2.30:1.3: Atha kho sambahulā nānātitthiyasāvakā devaputtā asamo ca sahali ca nīko ca ākoṭako ca vegabbhari ca māṇavagāmiyo ca abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ veḷuvanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.
root_text.sn2.30:1.4: Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho asamo devaputto pūraṇaṁ kassapaṁ ārabbha bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn2.30:3.1: Atha kho sahali devaputto makkhaliṁ gosālaṁ ārabbha bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn2.30:4.4: Na hi nūna tādisaṁ karoti pāpan”ti.
root_text.sn2.30:5.1: Atha kho nīko devaputto nigaṇṭhaṁ nāṭaputtaṁ ārabbha bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn2.30:7.1: Atha kho ākoṭako devaputto nānātitthiye ārabbha bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn2.30:8.4: Na hi nūna te sappurisehi dūre”ti.
root_text.sn2.30:9.1: Atha kho vegabbhari devaputto ākoṭakaṁ devaputtaṁ gāthāya paccabhāsi:
root_text.sn2.30:11.1: Atha kho māro pāpimā begabbhariṁ devaputtaṁ anvāvisitvā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn2.30:13.1: Atha kho bhagavā “māro ayaṁ pāpimā” iti viditvā, māraṁ pāpimantaṁ gāthāya paccabhāsi:
root_text.sn2.30:14.1: “Ye keci rūpā idha vā huraṁ vā,
root_text.sn2.30:15.1: Atha kho māṇavagāmiyo devaputto bhagavantaṁ ārabbha bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn2.30:0.3: The Disciples of Various Monastics of Other Religions
translation_text.sn2.30:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn2.30:1.3: Then, late at night, several glorious godlings lit up the entire Bamboo Grove. They were Asama, Sahalī, Niṅka, Ākoṭaka, Veṭambarī, and Māṇavagāmiya, and all of them were disciples of various monastics of other religions. They went up to the Buddha, bowed, and stood to one side.
translation_text.sn2.30:1.4: Standing to one side, the godling Asama spoke this verse about Pūraṇa Kassapa in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.30:2.5: What he taught should truly be trusted,
translation_text.sn2.30:2.6: he’s worthy of esteem as Teacher.”
translation_text.sn2.30:3.1: Then the godling Sahalī spoke this verse about the bamboo-staffed ascetic Gosāla in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.30:4.1: “Through fervent mortification in disgust of sin he became well restrained.
translation_text.sn2.30:4.2: He gave up quarreling with people.
translation_text.sn2.30:4.3: Refraining from false speech, he spoke the truth.
translation_text.sn2.30:4.4: Surely such a man does no wrong!”
translation_text.sn2.30:5.1: Then the godling Niṅka spoke this verse about the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.30:6.2: well restrained in the fourfold constraint;
translation_text.sn2.30:6.3: explaining what is seen and heard:
translation_text.sn2.30:6.4: surely he can be no sinner!”
translation_text.sn2.30:7.1: Then the godling Ākoṭaka spoke this verse about various sectarian teachers in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.30:8.2: as well as this bamboo-staffed ascetic and Pūraṇa:
translation_text.sn2.30:8.4: surely they weren’t far from truly good men!”
translation_text.sn2.30:9.1: Then the godling Veṭambarī replied to the godling Ākoṭaka in verse:
translation_text.sn2.30:10.3: A nude liar with suspicious conduct,
translation_text.sn2.30:10.4: though they teach a community, is not like the good.”
translation_text.sn2.30:11.1: Then Māra the Wicked took possession of the godling Veṭambarī and spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.30:12.1: “Those dedicated to mortification in disgust of sin,
translation_text.sn2.30:12.4: they rejoice in the heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn2.30:12.5: Indeed, those mortals give correct instructions
translation_text.sn2.30:13.1: Then the Buddha, knowing that this was Māra the Wicked, replied to him in verse:
translation_text.sn2.30:14.1: “Whatever forms there are in this life or the next,
translation_text.sn2.30:14.2: and those of shining beauty in the sky,
translation_text.sn2.30:14.3: all of these you praise, Namuci,
translation_text.sn2.30:14.4: like bait tossed out for catching fish.”
translation_text.sn2.30:15.1: Then the godling Māṇavagāmiya spoke this verse about the Buddha in his presence:
translation_text.sn2.30:16.2: Vipula’s said to be the best.
translation_text.sn2.30:16.3: Seta is the best of the Himalayan peaks,
translation_text.sn2.30:16.4: and the sun, of travelers in space.
translation_text.sn2.30:17.1: The ocean is the best of seas,
translation_text.sn2.30:17.2: and the moon, of lights that shine at night.
translation_text.sn2.30:17.3: But in all the world with its gods,
translation_text.sn2.30:18.5: The Linked Discourses on Godlings are complete.

variant_text.sn2.30:1.3: sahali → sahalī (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed) | nīko → niṅko (bj, pts1ed); niko (sya-all, km, pts2ed) | vegabbhari ca → veṭambarī ca (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn2.30:4.4: Na hi nūna tādisaṁ karoti → naha nūna tādī pakaroti (bj, cck, sya1ed, km, pts2ed); nahi nūna tādī pakaroti (sya2ed)
variant_text.sn2.30:10.1: Sahācaritena → sahāravenāpi (bj, pts2ed); saha racitena (sya-all); sagāravenāpi (pts1ed) | sigālo → siṅgālo (mr)
variant_text.sn2.30:17.2: nakkhattānaṁva candimā → nakkhattānañca candimā (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)

reference_text.sn2.30:1.1: csp1ed12.66, csp2ed12.66, dr15.90, ms12S1_556, msdiv111, pts-vp-pli1ed1.66, pts-vp-pli2ed1.152, sya15.95, vri23.81
